headline: Las Vegas Pre-Listing Checklist: The High-Ticket Fixes That Keep Showings Clean and Inspections Quiet
description: Las Vegas sits on shallow, well-drained, loamy soils with a petrocalcic (caliche) layer, so small movement can show up as door drift, corner cracks
abstract: Las Vegas sits on shallow, well-drained, loamy soils with a petrocalcic (caliche) layer, so small movement can show up as door drift, corner cracks, and fence/gate alignment—exactly the stuff buyers notice the second they touch an
articleSection: Related Guides You Might Find Helpful

mainEntity.name: What fixes move the needle most for showings?

acceptedAnswer.text: Doors that close clean, consistent wall finishes (no patch halos), and exterior water control (no stains/splash-back lines).

mainEntity.name: Why do patches “show” so badly in Vegas?

acceptedAnswer.text: Heat cycling and strong sunlight make texture/prime mismatches obvious—especially down long hallways.
Water paths that *look* minor—gutter overflow, discharge too close to the slab, or gaps at trim—because monsoon bursts expose them fast.

mainEntity.name: How do you keep a pre-list scope from ballooning?

acceptedAnswer.text: Bundle by system (water control, alignment, finishes), set clear boundaries, and plan a second “walk-through pass” at the end.

mainEntity.name: What’s the most common “Vegas tell” buyers notice?

acceptedAnswer.text: Doors/gates that don’t latch smoothly and exterior staining—little function problems that suggest bigger ones.
